findings.evidence: `rg -n "^abbrev GridState|structure GridDiagram|def pointSet" code/TauCeti/KnotTheory/Grid/Diagram.lean` shows `abbrev GridState (n : ℕ) := Equiv.Perm (Fin n)` at lines 43-44, while the public API only characterizes `pointSet`, rows/columns, cardinality, and disjointness.
findings.fix: Make `GridState` an opaque structure wrapping the permutation, expose only the needed coercion/application/extensionality and point-set API, and keep raw permutation operations behind explicit accessors if needed.
findings.issue: `GridState` is an `abbrev` for `Equiv.Perm (Fin n)`, so every permutation-group operation and instance becomes public API for grid states even though grid states are just permutation graphs for the roadmap target.

summary: The point-set lemmas are present, but the main type exposes too much implementation as public API.
